skill.name: qwen-image
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Qwen-Image-2.0 (fast), Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro (professional text rendering). Capabilities: text-to-image, multi-image editing, complex text rendering. Triggers: qwen image, qwen-image, alibaba image, dashscope image, qwen image 2, qwen image pro
skill.summary: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 53 potential issues (external_commands, network, weak_crypto, system_reconnaissance). Manual evaluation confirms all findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code formatting, URLs point to the legitimate inference.sh API service, and there is no cryptographic code or reconnaissance behavior. The skill is documentation for an image generation CLI tool with no malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Patterns in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected backtick commands in SKILL.md. These are markdown code formatting for example CLI commands (infsh app run...), not actual code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks are in markdown code blocks showing example commands users can copy. No eval() or exec() calls present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Point to Service API
low_findings.description: URLs detected (inference.sh, example.com) are legitimate service endpoints required for the skill to function as an image generation tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documentation links to inference.sh API service. No exfiltration or unauthorized connections detected.

content.value_statement: Users need a way to create and edit images using AI models directly from Claude Code. This skill provides seamless integration with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI for text-to-image generation, image editing, and professional text rendering.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Qwen-Image-2.0 and Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro models
Edit images using multi-reference input with the inference.sh CLI
Create images with professional text rendering using the Pro model
Control output with parameters: resolution, seed, negative prompts, watermark
Generate multiple images (1-6) in a single request
List and search available image models via inference.sh
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (infsh) installation and account
Network connection required for API calls to inference.sh
Image resolution constrained to 512x512 to 2048x2048 pixels

use_cases.title: Generate creative images from text
use_cases.description: Create unique artwork, illustrations, or photographs by describing what you want in a text prompt
use_cases.target_user: Content creators, designers, developers needing AI-generated visuals

use_cases.title: Add text to images for marketing
use_cases.description: Create posters, ads, and promotional materials with professional text rendering
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams, social media managers, small business owners

use_cases.title: Edit images with AI reference
use_cases.description: Edit or composite images by providing reference images for style, subject, or pose

prompt_templates.title: Basic image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of {description} using infsh app run alibaba/qwen-image-2
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to create a simple image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: High-resolution text poster
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a {text} poster with {style} style, {dimensions} resolution using Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro with prompt_extend: false
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need professional text rendering in an image

prompt_templates.title: Multi-image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate {number} variations of {description} using num_images parameter
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want multiple image options to choose from

prompt_templates.title: Image editing with references
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit image using reference images: {prompt} with reference_images from {image_urls}
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to combine or edit images using AI

output_examples.input: infsh app run alibaba/qwen-image-2 --input '{"prompt": "A serene mountain landscape at sunset"}'
output_examples.output: Returns generated images in PNG format with metadata including dimensions and count

output_examples.input: infsh app run alibaba/qwen-image-2-pro --input '{"prompt": "Poster with title \"Sale!\" in red bold text", "width": 1024, "height": 1536}'
output_examples.output: Returns a vertical poster image with rendered text, 1024x1536 pixels

best_practices: Use Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro for text-heavy designs and Qwen-Image-2.0 for general image generation
Set prompt_extend: false when you need precise control over the generated image
Specify exact dimensions within the 512-2048 pixel range to avoid resolution errors
anti_patterns: Do not use prompts exceeding 800 characters as they will be rejected
Avoid generating images outside the 512x512 to 2048x2048 pixel range
Do not forget to run 'infsh login' before using the skill

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run: npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools to install the infsh CLI tool

faq.question: Which model should I use?
faq.answer: Use Qwen-Image-2.0 for fast general generation. Use Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro for professional text rendering

faq.question: Can I generate multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes, use the num_images parameter (1-6) to generate multiple images in a single request

faq.question: How do I create reproducible images?
faq.answer: Set the seed parameter (0-2147483647) to get the same result from the same prompt

faq.question: Can I edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes, use the reference_images parameter with 1-3 input images for editing or compositing

faq.question: Is authentication required?
faq.answer: Yes, run 'infsh login' first to authenticate with your inference.sh account
